findings.evidence: Mathlib exposes heterogeneous `Diffeomorph.trans` at `Mathlib/Geometry/Manifold/Diffeomorph.lean:188`, and `rg "Diffeomorph.*trans_assoc|theorem trans_assoc" Mathlib/Geometry/Manifold/Diffeomorph.lean` finds no existing `Diffeomorph.trans_assoc`; the PR's only lemma is the self-case at lines 62-65.
findings.fix: Replace it with a public/general lemma, e.g. for `f : M ≃ₘ^n⟮I, I'⟯ M'`, `g : M' ≃ₘ^n⟮I', J⟯ N`, `h : N ≃ₘ^n⟮J, J'⟯ N'`, proving `(f.trans g).trans h = f.trans (g.trans h)`, then specialize it in the `Group` instance.
findings.issue: The private `trans_assoc` is specialized to three endomorphisms `M ≃ₘ^n⟮I, I⟯ M`, although associativity of `Diffeomorph.trans` is naturally heterogeneous.

summary: The group instance is at the right self-diffeomorphism level, but the PR proves associativity only as a private self-case instead of exposing the natural heterogeneous `Diffeomorph.trans_assoc` lemma.
